title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: 03-31-2026
content:
The credit union remains compliant in most areas with board-approved policy limits, supported by generally satisfactory asset quality and capital metrics. However, several efficiency and profitability categories fall below best-practice ranges.

Asset Quality:
Asset quality remains under pressure due to elevated charge-offs relative to the benchmark. Classified assets/Net worth of 0.04 (or 4%) remain within limit (< 40%), while delinquent loans/Total loans of 0.01 (or 1%) remain below its limit of < 1.5%. However, net charge-offs/Average loans of 0.01 (or 1%) exceed the best practice threshold of < 0.75%, indicating continued pressure in asset quality management.

Capital & Solvency:
Capital remains a key strength with solid solvency ratios. Net worth/Total assets of 0.14 (or 14%) remain above policy benchmarks, providing a substantial buffer against potential losses; however, net worth growth of 0.04% falls below the target of >= 5% YoY.

Profitability & Growth:
Profitability metrics remain mixed with some areas underperforming expectations. Net margin/Average assets of 0.04 (or 4%) and yield on average loans of 0.07 (or 7%) remain within policy guidelines, while net operating expenses/average assets of 0.03 (or 3%) exceed the limit of <= 3.00%. Growth metrics remain weak with loan growth at -5% below expectations for a range of 5%-10%, membership growth at -57% well below its target band of 3%-7%, and return on average assets at 1% falling short of the >= 80 basis points benchmark.

Interest Rate & Funding:
Liquidity and funding metrics remain within preferred ranges overall. Cost of funds/average assets (0.01 or 1%) remains below its limit, while net interest margin/Average assets (4%) is at target level; however, yield on average loans (7%) exceeds the minimum target but stays aligned with expectations.

Liquidity & Mix:
Funding mix metrics show mixed compliance signals. Cash and short-term investments/total assets of 19% are above range for an optimal band of 8%-15%, while regular shares/Total shares & borrowings at 44% fall below the >=70% target, indicating potential liquidity concentration risks.

Closing:
The credit union demonstrates strong capital buffers and controlled delinquency levels but faces challenges in profitability efficiency metrics. While asset quality remains generally acceptable with only one breach of charge-off limits, growth targets are significantly underachieved across loan portfolios and membership expansion efforts.
